title: Issue 2194 external consumer foundation lane

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Deliver issues #2195 through #2200 as a resumable stacked-PR lane, stopping broad readiness claims at the unresolved #2044 authority gate.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Preserve command IR as semantic authority, introduce no adapter lifecycle in AW, keep each child independently reviewable, and do not claim #2194/#2200 complete before #2044 permits it.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Extend operation conformance through the generated clients for #2198, then build the narrowed pre-#2044 independent-consumer proof for #2200.
proof_expectations: Run contract schema/tooling and generated-package freshness checks for each slice.
Run focused client, conformance, installed-boundary, and independent-consumer tests before publishing their owning slices.
completion_criteria: Issue 2194 external consumer foundation lane is implemented, validated, and closed out honestly.

goal: Ship the pre-#2044 external-consumer foundation in stacked PRs with durable, resumable planning state.
non_goals: Do not implement vendor adapters or the #2044 runtime-authority migration in this plan.
Do not close #2194 or claim broad adapter readiness while #2044 remains unresolved.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
